subject: Gaming
title: Early Access: Selling Unfinished Games
description: A micro-lesson that makes Early Access: Selling Unfinished Games usable.

hook.text: Pay full price for a game that's half done. Early access is controversial but kept indie studios alive.

buildup.text: Steam introduced Early Access in 2013. Developers could sell unfinished games and fund development.

discovery.text: Hades, Baldur's Gate 3, and Valheim used early access brilliantly. Player feedback shaped their success.

twist.text: Some games stayed in early access for years—or forever. DayZ spent five years in early access before 1.0.

climax.text: Early access works when developers communicate. Trust between players and creators makes the model viable.

punchline.text: Unfinished but funded. Trust makes early access work or fail.

quiz.question: What makes early access successful?
options: Transparent communication and trust between developers and players
